subject: Statistics
title: Real-World Evidence
description: A fast breakdown of Real-World Evidence for builders.

hook.text: Clinical trials use ideal patients in controlled settings. But your patients are messy, complex, and real.

buildup.text: Real-world evidence comes from electronic health records, insurance claims, and patient registries.

discovery.text: It reveals how drugs perform in actual practice — with missed doses, multiple conditions, and diverse populations.

twist.text: Real-world data is observational, not randomized. Confounders lurk everywhere, making causal claims risky.

climax.text: The FDA now accepts real-world evidence for some regulatory decisions. The definition of proof is expanding.

punchline.text: Lab conditions aren't real conditions. Real-world data fills the gap.

quiz.question: What is the advantage of real-world evidence over clinical trials?
options: It shows how treatments perform in diverse, real-world patient populations, not just ideal trial settings
It's always more rigorous than clinical trials
It eliminates the need for randomization
